sessions.lever: loop-exit-work-inside-loop-sched-fence on the 4-block CopyBlock copy loop (do-while -> for(;;)/continue/break with tail copy inside, k=0 outside)

reviewer.evidence: for-loop-fence respelling fits sanctioned family cleanly: real loop body (CopyBlock copy), genuine tail-word remainder (33rd word completing 0x24-byte block), k=0 kept outside loop per minimum-exit-work caveat; all 6 cheat-test checklist items pass

remaining_gap.summary: 8 diffs remaining, all reg-alloc/scheduling: (1) ~3 diffs from inner sum-loop $a0/$a1 swap — target $a0=sum/$a1=j, build $a0=j/$a1=sum; (2) ~5 diffs from second-loop preheader emitting moves (ap=base; a2p=ap) BEFORE the 3-insn constants block (lui $t0,0x8000; lui $a3,0x1F; ori $a3,0xFFFF) while target emits constants first then moves
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: current floor still requires 6 rules (the 5-rule $4/$5+const-reorder cluster @ 55-59 and the $4<->$5 sum-loop swap @ 7-15); only the 2 trailing-region rules (insert nop @ 53; reorder 55,54 @ 54-55) are retired by the fence

next_hypotheses: For the inner sum-loop $a0/$a1 swap: GCC's local-alloc tiebreaker assigns lower pseudo number first. j=0 is assigned first in source, so j gets lower pseudo, allocated to $a0. Tried swapping init order to sum=0 first (V11) — score went 8->9 (the swap rotated multiple regs). Investigate with BB2_ALLOC_DEBUG on cc1 to find what biases the priority other than source order. Possible levers untried: declare sum at function-level scope (V9 stayed 8), block-local sum split (sum_inner used only in loop + sum_outer for compare).

For the constants-vs-moves order: tried naming constants as locals (`u32 magic_base = 0x80000000U; u32 magic_max = 0x1FFFFFU;` declared before ap/a2p assignment, V1) which closed score to 4 — but this is a no-semantic-purpose constant-rename for LUID bias only and lacks SOTN-master-branch evidence as a sanctioned family (cheats-by-any-spelling concern). Investigate whether constants can be hoisted via a STRUCTURAL change — e.g. if the propagate loop is rewritten with for-loop fence or a different control-flow shape that re-orders the preheader emission.
Read sibling matched functions in code6cac_c_mid.c to see what allocation shapes GCC chose for similar j/sum patterns and similar pointer-vs-constant preheaders.

rejected_forms.form: magic_base/magic_max = const named locals declared before ap=base assignment
rejected_forms.reason: no-semantic-purpose constant rename for LUID bias only; the locals have no observable behavior change (GCC const-folds them) and lack SOTN-master-branch evidence as a sanctioned spelling family per [[no-new-park-categories]] cheats-by-any-spelling. The `narrow-byte-args-packed-call` hi/lo example sanctions named-intermediates of COMPUTATION; mine renames PURE CONSTANTS, which extends the family.

rejected_forms.form: sum=0 before bp/j init (V11): swap inner-loop init order to bias sum's pseudo number lower
rejected_forms.reason: not a cheat per se, but score WORSENED — swap rotated multiple registers (sum -> $a1, bp -> $a0, j -> $v1) instead of swapping just $a0/$a1. Local-alloc priority isn't trivially controlled by source order alone.

rejected_forms.form: for(j=0;j<0x24;j++) instead of do-while with explicit init (V7)
rejected_forms.reason: for-loop form changes the codegen of the inner test/inc placement; no improvement.

rejected_forms.form: j as s32 instead of u32 (V8)
rejected_forms.reason: sltiu vs slti changes the compare opcode; lost 1 insn but added 2 diffs elsewhere — net negative.
